display_name: MLflow Tracking Server / Model Registry (Artifact Store)
ai_scope_discriminator: The registry/run metadata IS the AI scope proof, not generic file/DB backup. Confirm in-scope without download by: (1) registered-models/list or registered-models/search returns a `registered_models[]` array with `name`, `latest_versions[]`, and per-version `source`/`run_id` — these are model-weight artifact pointers, definitionally ML; (2) model-versions/search returns `model_versions[]` whose `source` URI points at an artifact-root holding serialized weights — `MLmodel`, `model.pkl`, `model.pth`, `conda.yaml`, `requirements.txt`, `data/model.*`; (3) artifacts/list (metadata only) returns `files[]` with MLflow flavor artifacts (`MLmodel`, `model.pkl`, `pytorch_model.bin`, `model.safetensors`, `metrics`, `params`); (4) runs/search exposes `params`/`metrics` keys like `learning_rate`, `epochs`, `accuracy`, `loss` — training hyperparameters/eval metrics. Any one of these = confirmed AI/ML data (model artifacts, weights, training params). If artifacts/list returns only generic non-ML files and registered-models/list is empty, treat as substrate, OUT of scope per the AI-LLM-scope-not-substrate gate.
fingerprint_markers: GET /api/2.0/mlflow/registered-models/list returns {"registered_models":[...]} (200 with no auth challenge = unauth)
SPA index references static-files/lib/mlflow and bundle paths /static-files/
GET /version returns the MLflow server version string
JSON response keys registered_models / model_versions / latest_versions / artifact_uri / MLmodel / run_uuid are MLflow-unique
artifacts/list response field files[] with is_dir/path/file_size
Server/Werkzeug Flask response headers on /health and /version
fp_guards: Databricks-hosted MLflow (adb-*.azuredatabricks.net, dbc-*.cloud.databricks.com, *.gcp.databricks.com) serves the same /api/2.0/mlflow paths but is auth-gated SaaS — exclude by 401/403 on registered-models/list and by Databricks redirect/login page; only count self-hosted servers that return 200 with a JSON body.

Generic Flask/Werkzeug apps on port 5000 falsely match broad port:5000 dorks — require the MLflow-unique JSON marker (probe /ajax-api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/search returning {"experiments":...}) not just the port or a Werkzeug header.

Reverse proxies / SSO portals fronting MLflow return the login HTML instead of JSON — a 200 HTML page that lacks the registered_models/experiments JSON key is auth-fronted, mark surface-open-access-not-exercised, not unauth.
Catch-all / honeypot pages echoing 'mlflow' in http.title without the SPA static-files/lib/mlflow bundle or the JSON API contract are FP — anchor on the API JSON field, never on http.title:'MLflow'.
Kubeflow/Seldon/BentoML bundling an MLflow tab may share strings; confirm the bare /api/2.0/mlflow/ contract responds on the host itself.

cves.desc: Unauthenticated LFI / arbitrary file read via URI-fragment path traversal in the artifacts endpoint (GET .../artifacts/somefile#../../../etc/passwd); affects MLflow <= 2.11.2, fixed 2.11.3.
Authentication bypass: attacker can arbitrarily create an MLflow account, defeating basic-auth even when enabled (CVSS 9.5, CWE-598).
Unauthenticated arbitrary file write via the artifacts/model creation path, enabling overwrite of server files.
Path traversal in static/local file serving allowing read of arbitrary host files from the tracking server.
URI-fragment directory-traversal local file read variant in artifact path resolution.
Path traversal in MLflow allowing local file inclusion / read across the artifact-handling routes.
Pre-auth directory-traversal RCE: crafted POST model-version create with malicious `source` writes .py/.pth into the Python path -> arbitrary code execution as the MLflow service account.

pivot_paths: GET /api/2.0/mlflow/registered-models/list -> enumerate registered model NAMES + latest_versions (names only).
POST /api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/search {"max_results":1000} -> list experiment names/ids without touching artifacts.
POST /api/2.0/mlflow/model-versions/search -> per-version `source` artifact-root URIs (S3/MinIO/local path) to confirm where weights live, WITHOUT fetching them.
GET /api/2.0/mlflow/registered-models/get?name=<m> -> read model description/tags/stages metadata for the AI-scope discriminator.
GET /api/2.0/mlflow/artifacts/list?path=<dir> -> list artifact filenames + sizes (MLmodel/model.pkl present = confirm flavor), directory listing only.
POST /api/2.0/mlflow/runs/search -> read params/metrics KEYS (hyperparameters, eval metrics) to confirm training data without record exfil.
Read the `source` S3/MinIO bucket URI to note artifact-root exposure for a separate object-store entry — note the bucket name, do NOT enumerate or download bucket objects.
restraint_note: Names-and-metadata only. List endpoints (registered-models/list, experiments/search, model-versions/search, artifacts/list) return model names, version pointers, and artifact filenames — that IS the finding and the proof of exposure. Do NOT GET an artifact body, do NOT call get-download-uri then fetch the object, do NOT pull S3/MinIO objects named in `source` — that is exfiltration of model weights/training data. Never POST to model-versions/create, registered-models/create, or any path with a `source`/path parameter — those are write/restore primitives and the CVE-2025-11201 / CVE-2023-6018 RCE+write surface; sending them is an active write, strictly forbidden. CVE-2024-2928 LFI is confirmed by a single minimal /etc/hostname read at most, never broad filesystem traversal. Enumerate metadata, do not exfiltrate.
